overview: Edge of Eternities is a high-tech sci-fi set that takes place in the Sothera system. The format is defined by artifact synergies, the Void mechanic, and Spacecrafts. It features a mix of high-speed artifact aggro and grindy resource-based control decks.

mechanics.name: Void
mechanics.description: Use the void mechanic at the cost of creatures and artifacts to trigger devastating events.
mechanics.draft_tip: Draft cards that generate 'Scrap' or 'Drone' tokens to use as fuel for your Void costs without losing your main board presence.

mechanics.name: Lander Tokens
mechanics.description: Lander tokens can be sacrificed to put a land from your hand onto the battlefield or to trigger landfall.
mechanics.draft_tip: Essential for the RG Landfall archetype; they allow you to trigger landfall at instant speed during combat.

mechanics.name: Spacecrafts (Crew)
mechanics.description: Station your Spacecrafts by tapping creatures, then reap the rewards of your dedicated crew.
mechanics.draft_tip: Spacecrafts (Vehicles) are high-impact but need pilots. Ensure your deck has at least a 2:1 ratio of creatures to Spacecrafts.

archetypes.name: Second Spell
archetypes.description: Cast your second spell in a turn and earn additional bonuses to overwhelm your opponent.
archetypes.strategy: Prioritize cheap 1-mana spells and cantrips to consistently trigger your 'second spell' bonuses every turn.

archetypes.name: Artifact Control
archetypes.description: Assemble an artifact-based deck to grind out your opponent's resources turn by turn.
archetypes.strategy: Focus on removal and recursive artifacts. Win the long game by exhausting the opponent's options.

archetypes.name: Void
archetypes.description: Use the void mechanic at the cost of creatures and artifacts—then trigger devastating events.
archetypes.strategy: Aggressive sacrifice. Use small tokens as fodder to activate powerful Void abilities that clear the board or burn the opponent.

archetypes.name: Landers & Landfall
archetypes.description: Use Lander tokens and landfall to create mana advantage. Landers can help to trigger landfall more easily.
archetypes.strategy: Generate as many Lander tokens as possible. Use them to ramp into massive threats or trigger mid-combat landfall buffs.

archetypes.name: +1/+1 Counters
archetypes.description: Boost your entire army with +1/+1 counters—stack 'em up and watch the enemies fall.
archetypes.strategy: Build a wide board and use mass-pump spells. Ideal for overwhelming slower control decks.

archetypes.name: Go Wide
archetypes.description: Assemble the largest battlefield in the system and conquer your opponents with sheer numbers.
archetypes.strategy: Prioritize cards that create multiple bodies. Use 'Anthem' effects to make your swarm of Drones lethal.

archetypes.name: Artifact Aggro
archetypes.description: Play cheap artifacts you've uncovered on your travels to quickly overpower the field with supreme synergies.
archetypes.strategy: Fastest deck in the format. Use low-cost artifact creatures and 'Affinity'-like effects to dump your hand by turn 3.

archetypes.name: Graveyard
archetypes.description: Salvage long-forgotten ships and fallen warriors with a graveyard strategy that keeps you coming back.
archetypes.strategy: Self-mill is your friend. Use the graveyard as a second hand to out-value opponents in the late game.

archetypes.name: Space Stations
archetypes.description: Station your Spacecrafts by tapping creatures, then reap the rewards of your dedicated crew.
archetypes.strategy: Focus on high-Crew-value Spacecrafts. Use creatures that provide bonuses specifically when they crew a vehicle.

archetypes.name: Ramp
archetypes.description: Explore Sothera's furthest regions and worlds by controlling the largest monsters and ships in the system.
archetypes.strategy: Spend the first 3 turns developing mana. Once you hit 6+ mana, deploy 'Bombs' that the opponent cannot answer.

general_tips: Artifact removal is main-deckable in this set due to the high number of Spacecrafts and Artifact Aggro decks.
Don't ignore Lander tokens; they are the key to mana fixing in a 3-color Sothera strategy.
The format is fast. If your deck doesn't have a play on turn 2, you will likely lose to Artifact Aggro.
Spacecrafts are powerful but vulnerable to 'crew-kill' responses; always hold up protection when crewing.
